title: Apple strudel
subtitle: Spiced Bramley apple filling wrapped in buttered filo layers
excerpt: Bramley apples tossed with cinnamon, brown sugar, and breadcrumbs, rolled in layers of buttered filo pastry and baked until golden and shatteringly crisp. Serve warm with cream. Serves 6.
sourceNotes: Apple strudel (Apfelstrudel) is a traditional Austrian pastry documented from the seventeenth century, with the earliest surviving handwritten recipe from 1696 in the Vienna City Library. It became widely documented in German and Austrian household baking guides in the nineteenth century. The filo version here is the accessible home-baker's form; authentic strudel uses a hand-stretched dough pulled paper-thin. The Bramley apple is the British cooking apple used here in place of the traditional Winesap or Boskoop varieties.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best eaten warm on the day. Reheat at 180°C fan for 8 minutes.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 200°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: A strudel is rolled rather than folded. The buttered filo sheets are layered on a clean cloth or sheet of baking paper; the filling is spread leaving a border at the far edge and sides. The near edge is lifted and folded over the filling once to start the roll, then the cloth is used to coax the roll forward so the strudel rolls over itself. Fold in the sides as you go. The seam goes underneath. The breadcrumbs in the filling absorb excess moisture and prevent soggy pastry.

content.text: The breadcrumbs are not optional. Bramley apples release a lot of juice as they bake, and without the breadcrumbs to soak it up the pastry layers will be soggy underneath. Toast them first in butter so they add flavour as well as structure. The
looks difficult but the cloth makes it easy.

Toss the {{apple-bramley}} slices with the {{soft-brown-sugar}} and {{cinnamon-ground}} in a bowl. Toast the {{breadcrumbs-dried}} in the {{unsalted-butter}} (crumb amount) in a small pan until golden and nutty. Stir the toasted crumbs through the apple mixture.

Preheat the oven to 190°C fan. Lay a large sheet of baking paper on your work surface. Place one sheet of {{filo-pastry}} on the paper and brush with melted {{unsalted-butter}} (pastry amount). Layer on five more sheets, brushing each. Spread the apple filling in a log shape across the near third of the pastry, leaving a 4 cm border at the sides and the far edge. Fold the sides in. Using the paper, roll the strudel firmly away from you, seam-side down. Transfer to a lined baking tray. Brush all over with butter. Bake for 30–35 minutes until deep golden and the pastry is visibly crisp and layered. Cool for 10 minutes then dust generously with {{icing-sugar}}. Serve warm.
